description: ECG trace showing QRS widening from Class I antiarrhythmic drugs

strategy.thinking: The content focuses on the electrophysiological impact of Class I antiarrhythmics (sodium channel blockers) on ventricular depolarization. The queries target the specific drug class and the hallmark clinical finding (QRS widening) using technical terminology.
strategy.guidance: Select a professional ECG strip that clearly demonstrates a widened QRS complex characteristic of sodium channel blockade.

reasoning: This image provides a clear, professional schematic ECG trace that demonstrates the progressive widening of the QRS complex. It specifically illustrates the 'rate-dependent blockade' (use-dependence) characteristic of Class I antiarrhythmic drugs (particularly Class Ic), where the QRS interval increases as the drug binds more effectively to sodium channels over successive beats. This dynamic representation is more educational than a static wide QRS as it shows the transition from narrow to wide.
selected_image_description: A schematic ECG strip showing a sequence of heartbeats where the QRS complex progressively widens from left to right. Below the trace is a diagram illustrating the binding and unbinding kinetics of the drug, labeled 'onset kinetics' and 'rate-dependent blockade', explaining the physiological mechanism behind the observed ECG change.
